items.title: Dimension compatibility for AB
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Amatrix["A m x n"] --> compat["inner dimension n"]
  Bmatrix["B n x p"] --> compat
  compat --> Cmatrix["C = AB m x p"]
  Amatrix --> noteA["Rows of A: m"]
  Bmatrix --> noteB["Columns of B: p"]
  Cmatrix --> noteC["Result shape: m x p"]
items.caption: Matrix multiplication AB is defined when the number of columns of A equals the number of rows of B; the product has shape (rows of A) x (columns of B).

items.title: Matrix multiplication as composition of linear maps
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  xvec["x in R^p"] --> Bmap["B: R^p -> R^n"]
  Bmap --> Amap["A: R^n -> R^m"]
  Amap --> yvec["y in R^m"]
  xvec -.-> ABmap["AB: R^p -> R^m"]
  ABmap -.-> yvec
items.caption: The product AB represents applying B first and then A, so AB is the composed linear map from the input space to the output space.
